prompt: Write a warm greeting to a new colleague in five different languages (your choice). After each, add a one-line note about what's culturally implied by the register you chose.
response:
- English: Hi [Name]! Great to meet you—looking forward to working together.
  Note: Casual first-name basis reflects approachable, low-hierarchy professionalism common in many English-speaking workplaces.

- Spanish: ¡Hola, [Nombre]! Un gusto conocerte; me alegra que vayamos a trabajar en equipo.
  Note: Tuteo (tú) signals peer-to-peer warmth; in some countries usted is preferred until invited to switch.

- French: Bonjour [Prénom], c’est un plaisir de faire votre connaissance; j’ai hâte de collaborer avec vous.
  Note: Using vous maintains professional courtesy, while the enthusiastic wording keeps it warm.

- Japanese: はじめまして。[名前]と申します。これから一緒に働けるのを楽しみにしています。どうぞよろしくお願いいたします。
  Note: Polite business register emphasizes respect and harmony; set phrases convey humility and readiness to cooperate.

- Mandarin Chinese: 你好，[名字]！很高兴认识你，期待和你一起合作。
  Note: Friendly-neutral tone for colleagues of similar rank; avoiding 您/贵 keeps it warm without sounding distant.
